I'll search for this GitHub repository to verify the reference.
The search confirms that the GitHub repository `https://github.com/Howuhh/sac-n-jax` exists. The repository page shows the project title/description as "Single-file SAC-N implementation on jax with flax and equinox." The username/owner is "Howuhh", not "Alexander Nikulin" as the cited author.

Let me search for more information about who Alexander Nikulin is in relation to this repository.
Based on my web search results, I have found conclusive evidence about the authorship of the GitHub repository.

The automated validator correctly identified an author mismatch. My search found that:

1. The GitHub repository `https://github.com/Howuhh/sac-n-jax` exists and is confirmed to be owned/maintained by the user "Howuhh" (not Alexander Nikulin as the cited author)

2. However, the search also revealed that Alexander Nikulin is a real researcher in reinforcement learning who works at Tinkoff/AIRI and has published papers on SAC-N and offline RL (referenced in multiple academic papers)

3. Critically, from Alexander Nikulin's personal website (https://howuhh.github.io/), I found evidence that "Howuhh" is his GitHub username. His personal website shows references to projects and code repositories, and the portfolio mentions "Code" repositories.

The key finding is that "Howuhh" is the GitHub username for Alexander Nikulin. The repository exists, the title matches, and the author is correctly identified when accounting for GitHub username conventions. This is a simple metadata mismatch between a formal name (Alexander Nikulin) and a GitHub username (Howuhh), not a fabricated reference.

EXPLANATION: The GitHub repository exists at the cited URL with a matching title. The cited author "Alexander Nikulin" corresponds to the GitHub user "Howuhh" (his username), as confirmed by his personal website. This is a metadata mismatch between formal name and GitHub username, not a fabricated reference.
